case_name: Richard Wilkinson v. Washington Medical Commission

summary.brief: Richard Wilkinson v. Washington Medical Commission. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Dr. Wilkinson challenges discipline imposed on him by the Washington Medical Commission related to his treatment of seven patients with COVID-19
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Wilkinson for his website blogs breached his First Amendment free speech rights.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lancaster averred that he would not have prescribed ivermectin for these patients to treat COVID-19 because the antiparisitic drug was not an evidence-based treatment
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Scott Lancaster

evidence_index.text: Dr. Wilkinson did not document that he obtained informed consent from either Patient A or B regarding use of ivermectin.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Commission has proved by clear and convincing evidence that the Respondent has committed unprofessional conduct under RCW 18.130.180(4).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Despite disagreement with his views, I applaud Dr. Richard Wilkinson for enforcing at substantial cost his free speech rights.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge Fearing

evidence_index.text: Dr. Wilkinson prescribed a variety of medications, including ivermectin, zinc, and nebulized hydrogen peroxide.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I think the only bad side effect I’ve seen a patient have was an interaction with a medication that caused bleeding, warfarin
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: WMC ruled that Dr. Richard Wilkinson practiced medicine negligently
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Patients A and B, a married couple, were longtime patients of Dr. Wilkinson.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Fever for three days, concern for COVID-19
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patients A and B, a married couple, reported fevers for three days. Their daughter called the clinic expressing concerns about possible COVID-19 infection.

focused_exam.system: Examination not performed; consultation was via telephone

requestables.name: COVID-19 PCR Test
requestables.description: Polymerase chain reaction test for COVID-19

reveal.decision_impact: Positive result would confirm diagnosis and guide treatment

requestables.name: Informed Consent Discussion
requestables.description: Discussion of treatment options and risks

reveal.clinical_significance: Essential for patient autonomy and legal compliance
reveal.decision_impact: Would ensure patients are informed about treatment risks and alternatives

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patients A and B present with fever and suspected COVID-19

key_events.event: Daughter calls clinic reporting fever and concern for COVID-19

timeline_phases.name: Telephone Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Dr. Wilkinson conducts a telephone consultation with Patient B

key_events.event: Dr. Wilkinson prescribes ivermectin without seeing patients

decision_points.clinical_context: Patients A and B suspected of having COVID-19
decision_points.prompt: What treatment should be prescribed for suspected COVID-19?

options.description: Prescribe FDA-approved treatments such as monoclonal antibodies
options.clinical_reasoning: Monoclonal antibodies are effective and approved for COVID-19 treatment
Ivermectin is not FDA-approved for COVID-19 and lacks evidence of efficacy

expected_action_court.description: Monoclonal antibodies should have been prescribed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established monoclonal antibodies as standard

patient_outcome.description: Two patients ultimately died, others suffered moderate harm

key_findings: Breach of duty in prescribing non-standard treatments
Causation established - failure to recommend effective treatments

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to recommend standard COVID-19 treatments
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Standard treatments could have improved outcomes

factual_timeline.event: Patients A and B's daughter calls clinic with concerns of COVID-19

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide informed consent and prescribe appropriate treatment
Failure to recommend standard COVID-19 treatments

court_findings.finding: Dr. Wilkinson breached duty of care by prescribing ivermectin without informed consent
Dr. Wilkinson breached duty of care
